channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How young people are navigating a tough and rapidly changing job market
item.description: Finding a job is hard right now, especially for young people starting their careers. NPR's Adrian Ma spoke with college students and an economist about navigating today's tough job market.
item.content:encoded: Finding a job is hard right now, especially for young people starting their careers. NPR's Adrian Ma spoke with college students and an economist about navigating today's tough job market.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: Understanding why some Iranian Americans support the war on their country of origin
item.description: Protesters from the Iranian diaspora in the U.S. gathered in Washington, D.C. as the war in the Middle East broadens. Many say they are aligned with the U.S. and Israel and explain why they want to see regime change in Tehran.
item.content:encoded: Protesters from the Iranian diaspora in the U.S. gathered in Washington, D.C. as the war in the Middle East broadens. Many say they are aligned with the U.S. and Israel and explain why they want to see regime change in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Seeing the femme fatale as a full person: the hero and the villain
item.description: The femme fatale in the movies has held our attention since Hollywood's golden age but the archetype has evolved, say NPR staff in movies panel.
item.content:encoded: The femme fatale in the movies has held our attention since Hollywood's golden age but the archetype has evolved, say NPR staff in movies panel.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Controversy growing over buoys in the Rio Grande
item.description: Trump administration started deploying 500 miles of Big buoys in the middle of the Rio Grande, the dividing border line with Mexico, financed by 2025's spending bill. Locals aren't happy considering that illegal border crossings are at a 50-year low.
item.content:encoded: Trump administration started deploying 500 miles of Big buoys in the middle of the Rio Grande, the dividing border line with Mexico, financed by 2025's spending bill. Locals aren't happy considering that illegal border crossings are at a 50-year low.
item.dc:creator: David Martin Davies

item.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
item.description: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.
item.content:encoded: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Some critics of birthright citizenship say it's a fraud issue. What does that mean?
item.description: Advocates for ending birthright citizenship point to "birth tourism" schemes to argue that the legal principle is ripe for exploitation and threatens national security. Experts say it's not so simple.
item.content:encoded:
Advocates for ending birthright citizenship point to "birth tourism" schemes to argue that the legal principle is ripe for exploitation and threatens national security. Experts say it's not so simple.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Thousands of 'No Kings' protests take place around the world
item.description: Thousands of protests took place around the country today, against the Trump administration. We hear from people who came out about why they were there.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of protests took place around the country today, against the Trump administration. We hear from people who came out about why they were there.
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Ethics committee finds Florida Democratic representative guilty of violations
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found Rep. Shelia Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of ethics violations after a rare public hearing on allegations of financial crimes.
item.content:encoded: The House Ethics Committee found Rep. Shelia Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of ethics violations after a rare public hearing on allegations of financial crimes.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
item.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.
item.content:encoded: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: How will two landmark social media verdicts reshape how we use this technology?
item.description: Two landmark verdicts this week could reshape the way social media works. Aza Raskin, a co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, explains the implications of the courts' decisions.
item.content:encoded: Two landmark verdicts this week could reshape the way social media works. Aza Raskin, a co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, explains the implications of the courts' decisions.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma
